title: leptinella atrata subsp. luteola
intro: Leptinella atrata subsp. luteola, also known as small white woodrush, is a perennial herb that belongs to the rush family (Juncaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Leptinella atrata subsp. luteola include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and aid in wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
